elements.sent-2411-001: This study investigates the astronomical implications of the Ghosh-Kumar rotating Black Hole (BH), particularly its behaviour on shadow images, illuminated by celestial light sources and equatorial thin accretion disks.
elements.sent-2411-002: Our research delineates a crucial correlation between dynamics of the shadow images and the parameters $a$,~ $q$ and the $\theta_{obs}$, which aptly reflect the influence of the model parameters on the optical features of shadow images.
elements.sent-2411-003: Initially, elevated behavior of both $a$ and $q$ transforms the geometry of the shadow images from perfect circles to an oval shape and converges them towards the centre of the screen.
elements.sent-2411-004: By imposing the backward ray-tracing method, we demonstrate the optical appearance of shadow images of the considering BH spacetime in the celestial light source.
elements.sent-2411-005: The results demonstrate that the Einstein ring shows a transition from an axisymmetric closed circle to an arc-like shape on the screen as well as producing the deformation on the shadow shape with the modifications of spacetime parameters at the fixed observational position.
elements.sent-2411-006: Next, we observe that the attributes of accretion disks along with the relevant parameters on the shadow images are illuminated by both prograde and retrograde accreting flow.
elements.sent-2411-007: Our study reveals the process by which the accretion disk transitions from a disk-like structure to a hat-like shape with the aid of observational angles.
elements.sent-2411-008: Moreover, with an increase of $q$, the observed flux of both direct and lensed images of the accretion disk gradually moves towards the lower zone of the screen.
elements.sent-2411-009: Furthermore, we present the intensity distribution of the redshift factors on the screen.
elements.sent-2411-010: Our analysis suggests that the observer can see both redshift and blueshift factors on the screen at higher observational angles, while augmenting the values of both $a$ and $q$, enhancing the effect of redshift on the screen.
elements.sent-2411-011: Black hole, which is regarded as one of the most incomprehensible compact objects in the universe, have received a huge amount of observational evidence to present.
elements.sent-2411-012: In $2019$, the Event Horizon Telescope (EHT) has been instrumental in capturing the images of the super-massive BH at the heart of the galaxy Messier $87$ (M$87$), based on $1.3$ interferometric observations \cite{gkref1,gkref2,gkref3,gkref4,gkref5,gkref6 .}
elements.sent-2411-013: This is a substantial evidence of the presence of BHs, and it raises the compelling prospect of testing General Relativity (GR) in the future high resolution images of BHs.
elements.sent-2411-014: Moreover, EHT reported that the configuration of the magnetic field, surrounding the accretion disk, clarifying that the magnetically seized accretion disk is consistent with the predictions of general relativistic magnetohydrodynamic simulations models \cite{gkref7,gkref8 .}
elements.sent-2411-015: Recently, EHT released the first horizon-scale high resolution image of the Sagittarius (Sgr $A^{\ast}$), which is positioned at the heart of the milky way and showed that the angular size of the photon ring of the Sgr $A^{\ast}$ is consistent with the radius of the shadow critical curve, as calculated in GR within the range of $10\%$ nicely \cite{gkref9,gkref10,gkref11,gkref12,gkref13,gkref14 .}
elements.sent-2411-016: Under the shadow of these discoveries, it is reasonable to hypothesize that the accretion disk surrounding the BH could be influencing the observational characteristics of the BH shadows.
elements.sent-2411-017: Since observations are susceptible to astronomical environments around BHs, the intensity to which the current optical appearance of bright rings is enforced by the photon rings is still a debatable topic in the scientific community \cite{gkref15,gkref16,gkref17 .}
elements.sent-2411-018: Hence, it is necessary to analyze the physical properties of astronomical environments and their contributions to the BH shadows in more detail.
elements.sent-2411-019: As it is well-known in literature, a supermassive BH surrounding a radiating plasma results in a luminous accretion disk.
elements.sent-2411-020: The thermal synchronised electrons are thought to be the primary source of light in BH image investigation.
elements.sent-2411-021: Moreover, in the case of a high rotating BH, the emitted radiation can induce a phenomenon, so-called a relativistic jet \cite{gkref18 .}
elements.sent-2411-022: The jet's base is surrounded by the funnel wall, which can produce not only potentially triggers substantial amounts of thermal electromagnetic radiation, but also transforms the jet into a precise metamorphosis.
elements.sent-2411-023: In this scenario, the jet and accretion disc both contribute as essential background light sources that are vital to the BHs horizon-scale imaging procedures.
elements.sent-2411-024: It is widely acknowledged that in the surroundings of the rotating BHs, there prevails a photon region, which consists of orbits where photons move at a continuously constant radial distance, known as bound photon orbits.
elements.sent-2411-025: This photon region is also known as the photon sphere in the case of static BHs.
elements.sent-2411-026: When these orbits undergo a radial unstable perturbations, then the photons on contiguous orbits, that are not perfectly in the photon zone, may experience multiple half orbits around the bound orbit before either emerging or falling into the BH, are the so-called nearly confined bound photon orbits.
elements.sent-2411-027: And the photons that are close to the bound orbits manifest as an angular aspect on the observer screen, are almost near the critical curve, known as the photon ring \cite{gkref19,rtb30,gkref20 .}
elements.sent-2411-028: The possible observational properties of the BHs shadows and their related consequences were investigated for a long time.
elements.sent-2411-029: In $1979$, Luminet considered the radiation from a thin accretion disk surrounding the BH and obtained simulated images of a BH with thin accretion disk, which illustrates that the emergence of the BH shadow depends on the accretion flow and the outer edge of the central brightness depression \cite{gkref21 .}
elements.sent-2411-030: The shadow images for Kerr spacetime with Keplerian accretion disk have been discussed in detail in Refs.. \cite{gkref22,gkref23 .}
elements.sent-2411-031: Based on the general relativistic ray-tracing code, the authors in \cite{gkref24 investigated the shadow images of a geometrically thick and infinite thin accretion disk around the compact objects.}
elements.sent-2411-032: Recently, wang et al. \cite{rtb23 analyzed shadows of Schwarzschild BH surrounded by a Bach-Weyl ring with the help of the backward ray-tracing procedure.}
elements.sent-2411-033: The Bach-Weyl ring is a concentric thin ring that looks like a Newtonian ring of constant density, as introduced in \cite{gkref25 .}
elements.sent-2411-034: Inspired by these groundbreaking studies, many other theoretical constraints on BH shadows and some related geometrical properties are discussed in the community, including the shadows with different accretion flow matters and Einstein rings \cite{gkref26,gkref27,gkref28,gkref29,gkref30,gkref55,gkref56,gkref31,gkref32,gkref33 etc., based on theoretically well-motivated models.}
elements.sent-2411-035: Moreover, the BH shadow has also been analyzed with some interesting physical properties in \cite{gkref58,gkref59,gkref60,gkref61,gkref62 .}
elements.sent-2411-036: These heuristics configurations provide us with a realistic description of the shadow dynamics simulations as well as the influences of accretion disk on the BH images.
elements.sent-2411-037: The theory of GR is regarded as a successful theory only for explaining the gravitational interaction between submillimeter and solar system scales \cite{gkref34 .}
elements.sent-2411-038: However, Planck's length is generally expected to be replaced by the quantum theory of gravity with ultraviolet completion \cite{gkref35 .}
elements.sent-2411-039: Owing to the absence of fundamental quantum gravity, extended theories of gravity, as a phenomenological model representing a classical extension of the GR, were constructed by complying with the recent observational data and the data from local tests.
elements.sent-2411-040: Therefore, it is more interesting to analyze the accreting matter around the BHs in modified gravities.
elements.sent-2411-041: Hence, this study aims to deal with this gap in literature.
elements.sent-2411-042: In the fabric of modified theories, the charged BHs governed by nonlinear electrodynamics (NED) have recently obtained more significance with the development of astrophysical observations.
elements.sent-2411-043: This is the reason for the fact that astrophysical BHs are often carrying strong magnetic fields or immersed in a plasma medium.
elements.sent-2411-044: Therefore, it is interesting to investigate the intrinsic properties of BHs described by NED \cite{gkref36 .}
elements.sent-2411-045: In Maxwell's electrodynamics, there exists a singularity at the position of the point charge and has infinite self energy.
elements.sent-2411-046: To deal with this issue, Born and Infeld constructed a nonlinear electromagnetic field \cite{gkref37 .}
elements.sent-2411-047: Inspired by this pioneering study, the combination of GR and Born-Infeld field has been investigated to overcome the singularity problem of the BHs as well as some other properties see Refs.. \cite{gkref38,gkref39,gkref40 .}
elements.sent-2411-048: Moreover, in the action of effective field theory coming from superstrings, an extended Born-Infeld action occurs naturally as the leading part \cite{gkref41,gkref42,gkref43 .}
elements.sent-2411-049: From the astrophysical point of view, the influence of NED become quite important in super-strongly magnetized compact objects like pulsars and magnetars \cite{gkref44,gkref45 .}
elements.sent-2411-050: More importantly, the first regular BH solution was also investigated as a spherically symmetric solution to GR source by NED, known as Bardeen spacetime \cite{gkref46 .}
elements.sent-2411-051: Numerous publications devoted to investigate the intense properties of NED- sourced BHs (see for example Refs.. \cite{gkref47,gkref48,gkref49 ).}
elements.sent-2411-052: In this regard, Ghosh and Kumar \cite{rtb1 recently proposed a magnetically charged rotating BH model (known as GK BH), coupling the NED to gravity.}
elements.sent-2411-053: This BH has a similar type of singularities as illustrated by Kerr BH \cite{gkref50 .}
elements.sent-2411-054: The shadow dynamics cast by this BH has been discussed in \cite{rtb2 .}
elements.sent-2411-055: In addition, this BH spacetime is closer to Kerr-Newman BH \cite{gkref51 as compared to newly proposed NED triggered rotating regular BHs \cite{gkref52,gkref53 .}}
elements.sent-2411-056: More recently, the author in \cite{rtb6 considered the GK rotating BH and studied the scattering states of a massive scalar field and the superradiant amplification process.}
elements.sent-2411-057: With the above motivations, this paper aims to investigate the optical features of GK rotating BH spacetime with a spherical background light source along with a thin accretion disk.
elements.sent-2411-058: The paper will be completed in the following manner.
elements.sent-2411-059: In the upcoming section, we briefly define the review of GK rotating BH coupled with NED and then we investigate the effect of spacetime parameters on the BH shadow images constructed in celestial spheres.
elements.sent-2411-060: In the same section, we will discuss the simulated images of BH shadow within the spherical background light source, which are constructed by imposing the backward ray-tracing method.
elements.sent-2411-061: By considering geometrically and optically thin accretion disk, in section III, we will discuss the observed luminosity of the BH shadow images for values of the associated parameters.
elements.sent-2411-062: Furthermore, a comprehensive investigation will be conducted on the redshift factors for both direct and lensed images of the accretion disk in section IV.
elements.sent-2411-063: The last section is devoted to conclusions and discussions.

elements.sent-2411-233: In the last few years, the study of BH spacetime geometry with light rings and accretion disk has become increasingly popular among scientific community, particularly after the beginning of multi-messenger astronomy.
elements.sent-2411-234: The existence of accretion disks surrounding BHs is a well-substantiated phenomenon prompted by a wealth of empirical evidence.
elements.sent-2411-235: An important milestone was reached with the accomplishments of the EHT, which provided some deep insights into BH spacetime geometry.
elements.sent-2411-236: Particularly, the iconic depiction of a super massive BH within the M$87$ galaxy captured a photon ring of luminosity close to the event horizon, surrounded by an accretion disk.
elements.sent-2411-237: These observations revealed that the presence of a bright ring of radiation enclosing a central brightness depression provides the intrinsic properties of the plasma radiation near the BH.
elements.sent-2411-238: In this regard, the imaging process of BHs, the accretion of matter surrounded by high-energy radiation plays a significant role.
